title: Session Lifetime and Timeout Policy Enforcement
description:
**SECURITY GAP: Infinite sessions possible**

Stytch JWTs expire every 5 minutes but are auto-refreshed indefinitely. Without absolute and idle timeouts, a stolen session could work forever.

## Proposed Policy

- **Max session age**: 24 hours (absolute timeout from initial login)
- **Idle timeout**: 8 hours (from last activity)
- **JWT expiry**: 5 minutes (Stytch default)
- **Refresh allowed**: Only if under max age AND under idle timeout

## Per-Tenant Configuration

- High security tenant: 4-hour max, 1-hour idle
- Standard tenant: 24-hour max, 8-hour idle
- Low security tenant: 7-day max, 24-hour idle

## Session Metadata Storage Options

1. Stytch session custom claims
2. Cloudflare KV with session_id as key
3. JWT custom claims (signed, validated per request)

## User Experience

- Near timeout: Show warning banner "Your session will expire in 5 minutes"
- After timeout: Clear message "Session expired. Please log in again."
acceptance_criteria: Max session age (24 hours default) is enforced — sessions are revoked after absolute timeout
Idle timeout (8 hours default) is enforced — inactive sessions are revoked
Session refresh is only allowed within both max age and idle timeout limits
Session metadata (created_at, last_activity) is stored and updated per request
Timeout values are configurable per tenant
Expired sessions force re-authentication with clear user messaging
ux_changes: Session timeout warning banner before expiry. Clear error message on session expiration directing users to log in again.
middleware_changes: Session validation middleware extended with absolute and idle timeout checks. Last activity timestamp updated on each request. Timeout enforcement before session refresh.
data_model_changes: Session metadata storage (created_at, last_activity, max_age_seconds, idle_timeout_seconds) per session — either in Stytch custom claims or Cloudflare KV.
implementation_notes: Timeouts must be configurable per tenant to accommodate different security requirements. The session validation middleware (MBT-1275) must check both absolute and idle timeouts before allowing session refresh. Consider storing timeout config in the tenant directory alongside other tenant settings.
